arguments.content:
# S32-821314 Pemberton RFQ — Redline Strategy Update

## Status
Received supplementary TCs (Doc 08) post-RFQ. Material additions that **amplify** risks identified in initial assessment.

## Key Changes from Supplementary TCs

### 1. MFC Audit Provisions (PRICING RISK)
- **New:** Pemberton can audit supplier pricing vs. "comparable customers" up to **2x per calendar year**
- Audit conducted by 3rd-party benchmarking firm at Pemberton's nomination
- If audit shows supplier charged Pemberton MORE than lowest price in preceding 12 months:
  - Refund difference
  - Pay liquidated damages = 10% of excess
  - Bear Pemberton's audit costs
- **Implication:** MFC clause now has **enforcement teeth** — not just aspirational

### 2. Sub-Supplier Audit & Strategic Stock (OPERATIONAL RISK)
- Audit rights extend to **ALL sub-suppliers** and **principal sub-suppliers (>10% by value)**
- Sub-suppliers must grant equivalent audit rights to Pemberton
- **Strategic Stock Holding Requirements:**
  - Hold stock on Pemberton's behalf at Supplier's distribution centres
  - Minimum 4-week buffer of nominated critical SKUs
  - Pemb can demand rotation, audit annually
  - Supplier bears carrying cost for entire 3-year term
  - Strategic stock returned/destroyed at Pemberton's election on framework expiry
- **Implication:** New capital requirement (£2–5m depending on SKU mix) tied to Pemberton's demand

### 3. Change of Control (COC) Trigger (TERMINATION RISK)
- Existing MSA 18.2 allows termination if supplier has COC to competitor
- **NEW in Supplementary:** Pemberton notified within **5 BD** of ANY of:
  - >5% change in beneficial ownership
  - ANY change in senior leadership (C-suite or UK Managing Director level)
  - Any negotiation that could lead to COC within 12 months
- Failure to notify = breach; Pemberton can terminate without penalty
- **Implication:** Any M&A activity, private equity recapitalisation, or C-suite departure = termination risk

### 4. ESG/Rebate Linkage (MARGIN RISK)
- Rebate ladder percentages **reduced by 25% if Supplier fails annual ESG attestation** (see Compliance Annex)
- Material non-compliance = rebate reduction for that year + following year
- ESG attestation now includes Net Zero target validation and **SBTi status**
- **Implication:** ESG underperformance directly impacts rebate accrual (e.g., 4% rebate becomes 3% if ESG missed)

### 5. Cyber Insurance Requirement (NEW)
- **Add to MSA 12.1:** Cyber liability insurance £5m per claim / £10m aggregate
- Retroactive cover to Effective Date
- 24-month tail post-termination
- **Implication:** Additional insurance cost ~£50–80k/year

### 6. Continuous Improvement Cost Sharing (NEW)
- SLA clause 6.3 cost-out benefits now **split 50/50 with Pemberton** for Initial Term
- Previously stated as Supplier-borne initiatives with vague "quantified benefits"
- Now: Any supplier-initiated cost-reduction or efficiency gain triggers 50% claw-back to Pemberton
- **Implication:** Limits margin uplift from operational improvements

### 7. Order of Precedence Flip
- Supplementary TCs now **inserted immediately above GTCs** in precedence (MSA 20.1 becomes):
  1. Supplementary TCs
  2. GTCs
  3. MSA
  4. SLA
  5. Compliance Annex
  6. Pricing Schedule
  7. Orders
- **Implication:** Supplementary TCs override everything

## Redline Strategy: REVISED

### OLD #1 (Termination / Volume Floor): AMPLIFIED
**What changed:** CoC notification requirement triggers termination without cause. Your M&A or senior hire is now a termination event.
**New ask:** 
- Cap CoC notice to **ownership changes >25%** (not 5%)
- Exclude internal restructurings, secondary buyouts, and CEO-equivalent changes that don't affect control
- Require 60-day negotiation window before Pemberton can terminate on CoC grounds

### OLD #2 (Indemnity Cap): UNCHANGED
**Strategic note:** Still the #1 legal exposure. Precedence flip makes it harder (Supplementary TCs don't address indemnity, but they're now senior to the indemnity provisions). No change to ask.

### OLD #3 (Locked DPA): UNCHANGED
**Strategic note:** Still applies. Locked DPA is now even more important given compliance obligations are ESG-linked to rebates.

### NEW #4 (MFC Audit Cost): CRITICAL
**What triggered:** Audit provisions now have enforcement + cost recovery
**Ask:**
- Audit frequency capped at **1x per calendar year** (not 2x)
- Pemberton bears audit cost if differential < 2% (de minimis threshold)
- Disputes resolved through independent arbitration; loser pays
- MFC applies only to orders >£50k (exclude long-tail low-value SKUs)

### NEW #5 (Strategic Stock): DEAL MATERIAL
**What triggered:** New £2–5m capital requirement tied to Pemberton's unilateral demand
**Ask:**
- Strategic stock funded by **Pemberton on consignment basis** (Pemberton owns; Supplier manages)
- OR cap strategic stock at **2-week buffer** (not 4-week; reduces carrying cost by 50%)
- Supplier carrying cost capped at **prime + 2%** on landed cost of stock
- Strategic stock **excluded from inventory metrics** used for OTIF / SLA penalties

### NEW #6 (CoC Trigger): WALK-AWAY IF NOT FIXED
**What triggered:** Any senior hire or capital event becomes termination risk
**Ask:**
- CoC notice threshold: **>25% beneficial ownership ONLY** (remove leadership change trigger)
- 60-day good-faith negotiation window post-notice
- If agreement reached on continued supply, no termination
- **If Pemberton won't move on this, escalate to CFO: this kills PE funding / founder transitions**

### NEW #7 (Cyber Insurance): ADD-ON COST
**What triggered:** £5m cyber liability required
**Ask:**
- Cyber insurance **borne by Pemberton** (Pemberton is the target; Supplier is intermediary)
- OR cap Supplier cyber premium to **£30k/year flat** with Pemberton paying delta
- Cyber coverage tied to **Pemberton's specific data exposure** (not blanket £5m)

### NEW #8 (Continuous Improvement Clawback): MARGIN EROSION
**What triggered:** 50/50 split of cost-out benefits
**Ask:**
- Continuous improvement cost sharing applies **only to benefits >£100k in a calendar year**
- Supplier retains **first £500k of annual savings** (threshold + benefit floor)
- 50/50 share applies only to incremental savings above £500k
- **Or reframe:** Supplier absorbs cost-reduction investment; benefits accrue to Pemberton automatically (simpler)

### NEW #9 (ESG Rebate Linkage): COMPLIANCE RISK
**What triggered:** ESG failure = rebate reduction
**Ask:**
- ESG attestation failure **triggers 10% rebate reduction** (not 25%) in affected year
- Materiality threshold: only failures classified as "Material" per Compliance Annex (e.g., Modern Slavery breach; not process miss)
- Supplier has **90-day cure period** post-attestation notice before rebate reduction applies
- Net Zero target **validated against SBTi 1.5°C pathway** (not custom validation)

## Precedence Fix (CRITICAL)
**Supplementary TCs now at top of hierarchy.** This is a **poison pill** if Supplementary TCs include broad indemnity or liability expansions (they don't in this version, but it's a template risk).

**Ask:** Modify MSA 20.1 to state:
> "**Except as expressly stated below, in the event of conflict:**
> 1. Supplementary TCs (Doc 08)
> 2. GTCs (Doc 05)
> 3. MSA (Doc 02)
> 4. ...
> 
> **Notwithstanding the above, the following provisions shall always be read together and shall NOT conflict:**
> - MSA Limitation of Liability (11.1–11.3) — applies uniformly across all documents
> - MSA Indemnity cap (10.2 + 11.2 carve-out) — applies uniformly
> - Compliance Annex force-majeure (17.2) — applies uniformly"

This prevents Supplementary TCs (or GTCs) from silently amending liability / indemnity scope via precedence.

## Commercial Repricings Required

| **Item** | **Supplier Cost** | **Bid Impact** |
|---|---|---|
| Cyber insurance add | +£50–80k/year = £150–240k/term | +0.8–1.3% to ceiling prices |
| Strategic stock carrying (@ 2-week buffer) | +£400–600k/year = £1.2–1.8m/term | +6–10% to ceiling prices |
| MFC audit compliance overhead | +£50–100k/year = £150–300k/term | +0.8–1.6% to ceiling prices |
| ESG attestation (SBTi validation) | +£20–30k/year = £60–90k/term | +0.3–0.5% to ceiling prices |
| CoC / M&A legal (ongoing compliance) | +£30–50k/year = £90–150k/term | +0.5–0.8% to ceiling prices |
| **SUBTOTAL NEW COSTS** | | **+8.7–14.2%** |

**Key decision:** Can you absorb 8–14% cost uplift in pricing, or does this kill the bid economics?

## Next Steps

1. **Escalate to CFO / Commercial:** Strategic stock carrying + CoC trigger are material operational changes
2. **Insurance broker:** Confirm cyber insurance availability + cost
3. **Redline draft:** Once commercial approves, build marked-up Supplementary TCs inserting:
   - MFC audit limits (1x/year, cost-sharing)
   - Strategic stock reframing (Pemberton consignment OR 2-week buffer)
   - CoC threshold (25% ownership only)
   - Precedence fix (liability/indemnity explicit carve-outs)
4. **Bid pricing:** Factor 8–14% uplift into ceiling prices OR request volume commitment to offset
5. **Clarification window:** Due June 15. We have 20 days.

---

**Recommendation:** Supplementary TCs are **material enough to warrant CFO discussion before finalising bid strategy.** Strategic stock alone could be £1.2–1.8m over 3 years.

blocks.text:
You're right to flag this. The Supplementary TCs are **material enough to rewrite the commercial picture entirely.** Let me walk you through what changed and what it means for your redline strategy.

---

## **THE THREE CRITICAL CHANGES (In Order of Business Impact)**

### **#1: STRATEGIC STOCK CARRYING (£1.2–1.8M OVER 3 YEARS)**

**What Pemberton Added:**
- You must hold 4-week buffer of nominated "critical SKUs" at **your distribution centres, on your dime.**
- Pemberton can nominate which SKUs are critical (they control the list).
- Pemberton audits annually; can demand rotation.
- **You eat the carrying cost for the entire 3-year term.**
- On framework expiry, Pemberton returns it or destroys it at their election (you don't get residual value).

**Why This Kills the Bid If You Don't Fix It:**
- 4-week buffer on 1,247 SKUs across 7 categories at £18.4m annual spend = **conservatively £2–5m in inventory you're funding.**
- At 15–20% annual carrying cost (warehouse, insurance, obsolescence, capital), that's **£300–1,000k per year just in cost of money.**
- On 8% gross margin (£1.47m/year), you've just given away **20–65% of your margin to inventory finance.**
- **If you terminate early** (Pemberton's 30-day exit right), the 4-week buffer becomes dead inventory you can't sell.

**Your Play (Pick One):**

**Option A (Best for You):** "Strategic stock is Pemberton's consignment inventory. You own it, we manage and rotate it. You pay warehouse fees (£50k/year), we pay pick/pack. At framework end, you buy it at 80% of landed cost or we destroy it."
- Transfers ownership to Pemberton → it's their balance sheet, not yours.
- Pemberton won't love this, but procurement teams do: consignment inventory improves their working capital metrics.

**Option B (Fallback):** "Cap strategic stock at **2-week buffer**, not 4-week."
- Cuts carrying cost in half: £600–900k/year instead of £1.2–1.8m/year.
- Still material, but survivable at margin.
- Reduces obsolescence risk (faster rotation).

**Option C (If Pemberton Holds Firm):** Price the inventory carry into ceiling prices.
- £1.2m/3-year cost ÷ £55.2m total 3-year spend = **+2.2% uplift minimum.**
- But this signals to Pemberton in bid evaluation that you're expensive (they'll compare to competitors who don't account for it).

**Why This Matters for Your 5-Minute Pitch:**
"You're asking us to float £2–5m in inventory for 3 years with no return and no protection if you exit. That's not a supply arrangement — that's a vendor financing arrangement. Either fund it yourself (consignment), or let us cap it to what's operationally sensible (2 weeks), or let us price the cost into your order."

---

### **#2: CHANGE-OF-CONTROL NOTIFICATION (TERMINATION RISK)**

**What Pemberton Added:**
- Any change in **beneficial ownership >5%** requires notification within 5 business days.
- **Any change in senior leadership** (C-suite or UK Managing Director level) requires notification.
- **Any negotiation that could lead to a change of control within 12 months** requires notification.
- Failure to notify = breach → Pemberton can terminate the entire framework without penalty.

**Why This Is a Land Mine:**
- You're a mid-market company. If you fundraise, do an M&A, bring in a PE investor, or even hire a new CFO, you **trigger a termination right.**
- Pemberton doesn't have to exercise it, but you lose leverage. In a negotiation, they can threaten termination to extract a price concession.
- If you're acquired by a competitor (or even a generalist buyer), you're automatically out.
- **This kills any founder exit or institutional capital plan for 3 years.**

**Your Play:**
- **Hard negotiation point:** CoC trigger should apply **only to ownership changes >25%** (meaning change of control in M&A law terms, not a 5% stake purchase).
- **Remove the leadership notification requirement entirely** (internal org changes are not Pemberton's business).
- **For the M&A negotiation clause:** "Supplier shall notify Buyer within 10 BD of entering into exclusive negotiations for any transaction that would result in >50% ownership change. Buyer has 60 days to negotiate continued supply terms; if agreed, no termination."
- This gives Pemberton a seat at the table on big transactions, but doesn't weaponise minor capital events.

**Why This Matters for Your 5-Minute Pitch:**
"You've written a clause that lets you exit if we hire a new CFO or if we take on a growth investor. That's not a commercial risk — that's a political control clause. We'll agree to notify you if we're acquired or fundamentally restructured, but ownership changes and org moves are off-limits."

---

### **#3: MOST-FAVOURED-CUSTOMER AUDIT + COST CLAWBACK (PRICING PRESSURE)**

**What Pemberton Added:**
- Audit rights **up to 2x per year** to verify you're not charging Pemberton more than your lowest price to any "comparable customer" in the preceding 12 months.
- 3rd-party benchmarking firm conducts the audit (Pemberton nominates; you bear the cost).
- **If audit finds you overcharged Pemberton:**
  - Refund the difference.
  - Pay **10% liquidated damages** on the difference.
  - **Bear Pemberton's audit costs** (likely £20–50k per audit).
- **Combined exposure per audit:** If audit finds £500k overcharge → you pay £500k refund + £50k LD + £50k audit cost = **£600k on a single finding.**

**Why This Is Dangerous:**
- "Comparable customer" is subjective. One auditor might say a customer of similar size in the same region is comparable; another might say a customer with different service levels isn't.
- You could be selling the same SKU to Company A at £100 (long-term contract, high-volume commitment) and to Company B at £105 (spot purchases, short lead times). Pemberton auditor might say the £100 price is your "lowest" and demand Pemberton match it.
- **2x per year audits = perpetual pricing pressure.** You can't predict when they'll audit, so you must price defensively (wide margins) or risk constant clawbacks.
- The liquidated damages (10% of excess) turn this into a penalty regime, not just a price-matching regime.

**Your Play:**
- **Audit frequency:** Cap at **1x per calendar year** (not 2x). Reduces audit burden and costs.
- **Cost allocation:** "Pemberton bears audit costs if the audit finds a variance of less than 2% (de minimis threshold). If variance >2%, Pemberton bears 50% of audit costs; Supplier bears 50%."
- **Comparable customer definition:** "Comparable customer means a customer in the same region, with similar annual spend (±25%), taking delivery on similar terms (same lead times, packaging, shipping). Excluded: customers with custom specifications, bespoke pricing arrangements, or bundled multi-year commitments."
- **Liquidated damages:** "In the event an audit finds Supplier overcharged by >2%, Supplier shall refund the difference plus 5% interest (not 10% LD). No additional clawback or penalty."
- **Dispute resolution:** "Any dispute over audit findings is resolved through independent arbitration; losing party pays arbitration costs."

**Why This Matters for Your 5-Minute Pitch:**
"MFC is reasonable — you want price parity with our other customers. But 2x annual audits with 10% penalties turns this into a pricing entrapment regime. We'll accept 1x annual audit with a 2% variance tolerance and reasonable dispute resolution. Anything else, we price a 3–4% risk premium into ceiling prices."

---

## **THE PRECEDENCE TRAP (Most Dangerous)**

**What Pemberton Did:**
The Supplementary TCs are **now at the top of the precedence hierarchy,** above the GTCs. Your original MSA 20.1 said:

> GTCs > MSA > SLA > Compliance > Orders

**The Supplementary TCs flip it to:**

> **Supplementary TCs > GTCs > MSA > SLA > Compliance > Orders**

**Why This Matters:**
If Pemberton ever amends the Supplementary TCs to add new indemnity carve-outs, new liability exclusions, or new compliance obligations, **those amendments sit at the top of the hierarchy and override the MSA caps you negotiated.**

Example: If Pemberton adds a new clause saying "Supplier indemnity for IP infringement is uncapped," that clause now **overrides MSA 11.2** (your indemnity cap) because Supplementary TCs are senior.

**Your Fix:**
Amend MSA 20.1 to include an explicit carve-out:

> "**Order of Precedence.** Except as expressly provided in this clause 20.2, the order of precedence for interpreting any conflict is: (i) Supplementary TCs; (ii) GTCs; (iii) MSA; (iv) SLA; (v) Compliance Annex; (vi) Pricing Schedule; (vii) Orders.
>
> **Notwithstanding the foregoing, the following provisions shall apply uniformly across all documents and shall not be overridden by any other document:**
> - **Limitation of Liability (MSA 11.1–11.3)** — Supplier's aggregate liability capped at 12 months' fees, except for carve-outs in 11.3.
> - **Indemnity scope (MSA 10 + 11.2 carve-out)** — Indemnity applies to [X, Y, Z] and is uncapped only for these triggers; all other indemnities are subject to the 12-month cap.
> - **Force Majeure (MSA 17)** — Neither Party liable for events beyond reasonable control.
>
> **Any conflict with these carved-out provisions shall be resolved in favor of the carved-out language.**"

This prevents a sneaky precedence flip from expanding your indemnity or liability.

---

## **COMMERCIAL COST IMPACT: THE DECISION POINT**

The Supplementary TCs add **6–14% in costs,** depending on what you negotiate:

| **Item** | **Cost If Unmitigated** | **Cost If Negotiated** |
|---|---|---|
| Strategic stock carrying (4-week) | +£1.2–1.8m/3yr (+6–10%) | +£0.6–0.9m/3yr (+3–5%) if 2-week, or £0 if consignment |
| MFC audit + clawback (2x/year, 10% LD) | +£200–300k/3yr (anticipatory pricing) | +£50–100k/3yr with 1x/year + 2% tolerance |
| Cyber insurance | +£150–240k/3yr | +£90–150k/3yr if capped at £30k/year |
| ESG rebate linkage (25% cut) | +£100–150k/3yr margin loss | +£30–60k/3yr if 10% cut + materiality threshold |
| CoC legal/compliance | +£90–150k/3yr | +£30–50k/3yr if threshold raised to 25% |
| **TOTAL** | **+£1.74–2.74m/3yr (+8–14%)** | **+£0.8–1.25m/3yr (+4–7%)** |

**Decision:**
- **If you can absorb 4–7% cost uplift:** Negotiate hard on #1–3 (strategic stock, MFC audit, CoC). These are the highest-ROI fixes.
- **If you cannot:** Walk. This bid doesn't work at +8–14% cost burden on £1.47m annual margin.

---

## **REVISED 5-MINUTE PITCH TO COMMERCIAL**

"Pemberton sent supplementary TCs that reframe the deal financially. Three things are material:

**#1 — Strategic Stock (£1.2–1.8m exposure):** They want us to float 4 weeks of inventory at our cost with zero residual value. That's 40–65% of our annual margin just sitting on our shelves. We ask them to either (a) fund it as consignment, or (b) cap it to 2 weeks (cuts cost in half). If they won't move, we price it in and the bid gets 6–10% more expensive. They'll see that delta and price us out.

**#2 — Change of Control (Termination risk):** Any capital event or senior hire gives them a termination right. This kills any growth capital or M&A plan for 3 years. We push back: CoC notification only applies to ownership changes >25% and senior leadership changes are off-limits. 60-day negotiation window if we do a deal. If they won't move, this is a walk-away — you can't run the business under this sword of Damocles.

**#3 — MFC Audit (Pricing entrapment):** 2x per year audits with 10% liquidated damages on any overcharge. That's a penalty regime, not price-matching. We ask: 1x per year, 2% variance tolerance, 50/50 cost-sharing, independent arbitration for disputes. This is standard. If they hold at 2x audits + 10% LD, we price in a 3–4% risk premium for constant clawback exposure.

**Bottom line:** Supplementary TCs add 8–14% in costs. If we negotiate hard on strategic stock and CoC, we can get to 4–7%, which is survivable. If Pemberton won't budge, we either absorb £2.7m in uncompensated costs over 3 years, or we walk.

**Recommendation:** Get commercial buy-in on the CoC pushback (deal-killer if not fixed) and the strategic stock reframing (consignment or 2-week cap). I'll redline the Supplementary TCs accordingly. We have 20 days."

---

**What do you want to do? Build the marked-up redlines on all three, or escalate CoC to CFO first?**
Mark up the Master Supply Agreement (02-master-supply-agreement.docx) with our redline based on your analysis. Save the output to the outputs/ subfolder of this matter.

I'll build a comprehensive redline on the MSA incorporating all three major negotiation points from my analysis. Let me start by reading the full MSA again to map exact clause locations, then prepare a marked-up version.

content.text:
> **File Path:** `/root/Documents/Oscar GC/Commercial/S32-821314 Sprint 30 — Pemberton RFQ pack + redline c06/02-master-supply-agreement.docx`

# Master Supply Agreement (Draft for Bidder Mark-Up)

Pemberton Engineering Ltd. — Indirect MRO Framework — 3-Year Term

**THIS MASTER SUPPLY AGREEMENT** is made on [DATE] 2027

**BETWEEN**

* **PEMBERTON ENGINEERING LTD.**, a company incorporated in England and Wales with registered number 03741892 whose registered office is at Unit 14, Trafford Park Industrial Estate, Manchester M17 1RH (“**Buyer**”); and

* **[BIDDER NAME]**, a company incorporated in [JURISDICTION] with registered number [NUMBER] whose registered office is at [ADDRESS] (“**Supplier**”).

(each a “**Party**” and together the “**Parties**”).

## 1. Definitions and Interpretation

1.1 In this Agreement, the following definitions apply:

* “**Affiliate**” means, in relation to a person, any entity that controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with that person.

* “**Background IP**” means Intellectual Property Rights owned or licensed by a Party as at the Effective Date.

* “**Confidential Information**” has the meaning given in clause 14.

* “**Effective Date**” means 1 January 2027.

* “**Foreground IP**” means Intellectual Property Rights created or developed in the performance of this Agreement, whether solely by a Party or jointly.

* “**Goods**” means the goods described in the Pricing Schedule, as may be amended from time to time.

* “**GTCs**” means Buyer’s General Terms and Conditions of Purchase as in force from time to time.

* “**Intellectual Property Rights**” or “**IPR**” means all patents, copyrights, design rights, trade marks, trade secrets and other intellectual or industrial property rights worldwide whether registered or unregistered.

* “**Order**” means a purchase order issued by Buyer for Goods.

* “**Services**” means the related services described in the Pricing Schedule or any Order.

* “**Term**” has the meaning given in clause 3.

1.2 Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation.

1.3 Where any conflict arises between this Agreement and the GTCs, the **GTCs shall prevail**.

## 2. Appointment

2.1 Buyer appoints Supplier on a non-exclusive basis to supply the Goods and Services during the Term.

2.2 Buyer makes no commitment to any minimum volume or value of Orders. Forecasts provided by Buyer are non-binding indications only.

## 3. Term

3.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of **three (3) years** unless terminated earlier in accordance with clause 18 (the “**Initial Term**”).

3.2 The Initial Term may be extended for a further period of up to two (2) years by mutual written agreement, such extension to be on the same terms as this Agreement unless the Parties agree otherwise in writing.

## 4. Orders

4.1 Buyer may issue Orders by any reasonable means including electronically. Each Order shall be deemed to incorporate this Agreement and the GTCs.

4.2 Supplier shall acknowledge each Order within two (2) Business Days. Failure to acknowledge within that period shall constitute acceptance of the Order on the terms set out therein.

4.3 Buyer may cancel or amend any Order at any time prior to despatch without liability to Supplier other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of cancellation, capped at fifteen percent (15%) of the Order value.

## 5. Pricing and Rebates

5.1 Prices are as set out in the Pricing Schedule and are exclusive of VAT.

5.2 Prices are firm for the first twelve (12) months of the Term. Thereafter Supplier may propose annual price adjustments capped at the lower of UK CPI and 2.5%. Buyer shall have absolute discretion to accept or reject any proposed adjustment.

5.3 Rebates shall accrue and be paid as set out in the Pricing Schedule.

5.4 Buyer reserves the right to benchmark prices at any time and to require Supplier to match any verified third-party offer for substantially comparable Goods.

## 6. Invoicing and Payment

6.1 Supplier shall invoice Buyer on a monthly basis in arrears.

6.2 Payment terms are **ninety (90) days** from receipt of a valid undisputed invoice.

6.3 Where any sum is disputed, payment of the disputed portion may be withheld pending resolution; the undisputed balance shall be paid in accordance with clause 6.2.

6.4 Interest on overdue undisputed sums shall accrue at the statutory rate under the Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998. The parties agree that this rate represents a substantial remedy under section 8 of that Act.

## 7. Delivery and Risk

7.1 Delivery shall be Incoterms 2020 DDP to the receiving site nominated in the Order.

7.2 Risk in the Goods passes to Buyer on signed acceptance at the receiving site. Title passes on payment in full in accordance with clause 6.

7.3 Supplier shall meet the service levels in the SLA. Failure to meet OTIF targets in any three (3) consecutive months shall constitute material breach for the purposes of clause 18.2.

## 8. Quality and Acceptance

8.1 Goods shall conform to the specification, applicable industry standards, and any sample provided.

8.2 Buyer may inspect and test Goods at any reasonable time. Non-conforming Goods may be rejected within thirty (30) days of delivery, at Supplier’s cost and risk.

8.3 Supplier warrants Goods against defects in materials and workmanship for a period of twenty-four (24) months from acceptance.

## 9. Intellectual Property

9.1 Each Party retains ownership of its Background IP.

9.2 **All Foreground IP** — including any modifications, improvements, derivatives, or jointly developed materials arising in connection with this Agreement — **shall vest in Buyer absolutely on creation**. Supplier hereby assigns to Buyer with full title guarantee all such Foreground IP, including future rights.

9.3 Supplier shall, at Buyer’s cost, do all such acts and execute all such documents as Buyer may reasonably require to perfect Buyer’s title.

9.4 Supplier grants Buyer a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free, worldwide, sublicensable licence to use Supplier’s Background IP to the extent embedded in or required to exploit any Goods or Foreground IP.

## 10. Indemnity

10.1 **Supplier shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Buyer, its Affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees and agents** from and against any and all claims, losses, damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable legal fees on a full indemnity basis) arising out of or in connection with:

* any breach by Supplier of this Agreement;

* any negligent act or omission of Supplier or its personnel;

* any defect in the Goods or Services;

* any infringement or alleged infringement of any third-party Intellectual Property Right; and

* any breach by Supplier of any applicable law or regulation.

10.2 The indemnities in this clause 10 are uncapped and survive termination.

## 11. Limitation of Liability

11.1 Subject always to clause 10 (which is uncapped), Supplier’s aggregate liability under or in connection with this Agreement and any Order, whether in contract, tort (including negligence), misrepresentation or otherwise, shall not exceed an amount equal to the fees paid or payable by Buyer to Supplier in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

11.2 **Buyer’s aggregate liability** under or in connection with this Agreement, howsoever arising, **shall in no event exceed £100,000.**

11.3 Neither Party shall be liable for indirect, consequential or pure economic loss; provided that this exclusion shall not apply to (i) Supplier’s indemnity obligations under clause 10, (ii) breach of confidentiality, or (iii) breach of clauses 13, 15 or 16.

## 12. Insurance

12.1 Supplier shall maintain at its own cost throughout the Term and for six (6) years thereafter:

* public and product liability insurance of not less than £10 million per claim and in the aggregate;

* professional indemnity insurance of not less than £5 million per claim and in the aggregate; and

* employer’s liability insurance to the statutory minimum.

12.2 Supplier shall provide certificates of insurance on request and shall give Buyer thirty (30) days’ notice of any material change to its cover.

## 13. Compliance

13.1 Supplier shall comply with the Compliance and ESG Annex (Document 06), which forms part of this Agreement.

13.2 Supplier represents and warrants that:

* it complies with the Modern Slavery Act 2015 and maintains a current modern slavery statement;

* it complies with the Bribery Act 2010 and maintains adequate procedures within the meaning of section 7 of that Act;

* it complies with all applicable sanctions, export control and anti-money-laundering laws;

* neither it, its Affiliates, nor any of its personnel involved in performing this Agreement is a Sanctioned Person or located in a Sanctioned Jurisdiction; and

* it maintains and shall maintain an environmental management system certified to ISO 14001 or equivalent.

13.3 Supplier shall provide an annual ESG attestation in the form prescribed in the Compliance Annex, signed at director level.

## 14. Confidentiality

14.1 Each Party shall treat the other’s Confidential Information as confidential and use it only for the purposes of this Agreement.

14.2 The obligations of confidence in clause 14.1 shall continue indefinitely.

14.3 The confidentiality obligations shall not apply to information that is (i) in the public domain through no fault of the receiving Party; (ii) lawfully received from a third party without restriction; or (iii) required to be disclosed by law or regulatory authority.

## 15. Data Protection

15.1 The Parties shall comply with the data processing terms set out in the Data Processing Annex available on request from Buyer’s Data Protection Officer, which shall be deemed to be incorporated into this Agreement on its current terms from time to time.

## 16. Audit Rights

16.1 Buyer may, on giving five (5) Business Days’ written notice, audit Supplier’s records, premises, systems and personnel insofar as they relate to Supplier’s performance of this Agreement, compliance with clause 13, or pricing under clause 5.

16.2 Audits may be conducted by Buyer or by a third-party auditor of Buyer’s choosing. Supplier shall provide reasonable cooperation and access at its own cost.

16.3 Audit rights survive termination for a period of six (6) years.

## 17. Force Majeure

17.1 Neither Party shall be liable for failure or delay in performance caused by an event beyond its reasonable control, provided that the affected Party gives prompt written notice and uses reasonable efforts to mitigate.

17.2 If a force majeure event continues for more than thirty (30) days, **Buyer** (but not Supplier) may terminate this Agreement on written notice without liability.

## 18. Termination

18.1 **Termination for Convenience.** Buyer may terminate this Agreement or any Order at any time **without cause** on **thirty (30) days’ written notice** to Supplier. No early-termination charges shall be payable other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of termination, capped at five percent (5%) of the value of work-in-progress on the termination date.

18.2 **Termination for Cause.** Either Party may terminate this Agreement on written notice if the other Party (i) commits a material breach that is not remediable, or is remediable but is not remedied within thirty (30) days of written notice; (ii) becomes insolvent; or (iii) undergoes a change of control to a competitor of Buyer.

18.3 On termination Supplier shall, at no charge, transfer to Buyer or a nominated replacement supplier all data, materials, and Foreground IP relating to the Goods and Services.

## 19. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

19.1 This Agreement and any dispute arising out of or in connection with it shall be governed by **English law**.

19.2 The Parties submit to the **exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Manchester**, England.

## 20. General

20.1 **Order of precedence.** In the event of any conflict between (a) this Agreement, (b) the GTCs, (c) the SLA, (d) the Compliance Annex, or (e) any Order, the order of precedence shall be (b), (a), (d), (c), (e). For the avoidance of doubt, **the GTCs prevail over this Agreement**.

20.2 **Assignment.** Buyer may assign this Agreement or any rights under it to any Affiliate or to any successor in business. Supplier may not assign or subcontract without Buyer’s prior written consent.

20.3 **Variation.** No variation is effective unless in writing and signed by an authorised representative of each Party.

20.4 **Entire agreement.** This Agreement, together with the documents referred to in clause 20.1, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties.

20.5 **Notices.** Notices shall be given in writing to the addresses set out at the head of this Agreement.

20.6 **Third-party rights.** A person who is not a Party has no right under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999 to enforce any term of this Agreement, save that Affiliates of Buyer may enforce clauses 9, 10, 13, 14, 15 and 16.

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF** the Parties have executed this Agreement on the date set out at the head of this Agreement.

Signed for and on behalf of **Pemberton Engineering Ltd.**:

Name: Title: Date:

Signed for and on behalf of **[BIDDER NAME]**:

Name: Title: Date:

---

2.2 Buyer makes no commitment to any minimum volume or value of Orders. Forecasts provided by Buyer are non-binding indications only.

## 3. Term

3.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of **three (3) years** unless terminated earlier in accordance with clause 18 (the “**Initial Term**”).

3.2 The Initial Term may be extended for a further period of up to two (2) years by mutual written agreement, such extension to be on the same terms as this Agreement unless the Parties agree otherwise in writing.

## 4. Orders

4.1 Buyer may issue Orders by any reasonable means including electronically. Each Order shall be deemed to incorporate this Agreement and the GTCs.

4.2 Supplier shall acknowledge each Order within two (2) Business Days. Failure to acknowledge within that period shall constitute acceptance of the Order on the terms set out therein.

4.3 Buyer may cancel or amend any Order at any time prior to despatch without liability to Supplier other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of cancellation, capped at fifteen percent (15%) of the Order value.

## 5. Pricing and Rebates

5.1 Prices are as set out in the Pricing Schedule and are exclusive of VAT.

5.2 Prices are firm for the first twelve (12) months of the Term. Thereafter Supplier may propose annual price adjustments capped at the lower of UK CPI and 2.5%. Buyer shall have absolute discretion to accept or reject any proposed adjustment.

5.3 Rebates shall accrue and be paid as set out in the Pricing Schedule.

5.4 Buyer reserves the right to benchmark prices at any time and to require Supplier to match any verified third-party offer for substantially comparable Goods.

## 6. Invoicing and Payment

6.1 Supplier shall invoice Buyer on a monthly basis in arrears.

6.2 Payment terms are **ninety (90) days** from receipt of a valid undisputed invoice.

6.3 Where any sum is disputed, payment of the disputed portion may be withheld pending resolution; the undisputed balance shall be paid in accordance with clause 6.2.

6.4 Interest on overdue undisputed sums shall accrue at the statutory rate under the Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998. The parties agree that this rate represents a substantial remedy under section 8 of that Act.

## 7. Delivery and Risk

7.1 Delivery shall be Incoterms 2020 DDP to the receiving site nominated in the Order.

7.2 Risk in the Goods passes to Buyer on signed acceptance at the receiving site. Title passes on payment in full in accordance with clause 6.

7.3 Supplier shall meet the service levels in the SLA. Failure to meet OTIF targets in any three (3) consecutive months shall constitute material breach for the purposes of clause 18.2.

## 8. Quality and Acceptance

8.1 Goods shall conform to the specification, applicable industry standards, and any sample provided.

8.2 Buyer may inspect and test Goods at any reasonable time. Non-conforming Goods may be rejected within thirty (30) days of delivery, at Supplier’s cost and risk.

8.3 Supplier warrants Goods against defects in materials and workmanship for a period of twenty-four (24) months from acceptance.

## 9. Intellectual Property

9.1 Each Party retains ownership of its Background IP.

9.2 **All Foreground IP** — including any modifications, improvements, derivatives, or jointly developed materials arising in connection with this Agreement — **shall vest in Buyer absolutely on creation**. Supplier hereby assigns to Buyer with full title guarantee all such Foreground IP, including future rights.

9.3 Supplier shall, at Buyer’s cost, do all such acts and execute all such documents as Buyer may reasonably require to perfect Buyer’s title.

9.4 Supplier grants Buyer a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free, worldwide, sublicensable licence to use Supplier’s Background IP to the extent embedded in or required to exploit any Goods or Foreground IP.

## 10. Indemnity

10.1 **Supplier shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Buyer, its Affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees and agents** from and against any and all claims, losses, damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable legal fees on a full indemnity basis) arising out of or in connection with:

* any breach by Supplier of this Agreement;

* any negligent act or omission of Supplier or its personnel;

* any defect in the Goods or Services;

* any infringement or alleged infringement of any third-party Intellectual Property Right; and

* any breach by Supplier of any applicable law or regulation.

10.2 The indemnities in this clause 10 are uncapped and survive termination.

## 11. Limitation of Liability

11.1 Subject always to clause 10 (which is uncapped), Supplier’s aggregate liability under or in connection with this Agreement and any Order, whether in contract, tort (including negligence), misrepresentation or otherwise, shall not exceed an amount equal to the fees paid or payable by Buyer to Supplier in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

11.2 **Buyer’s aggregate liability** under or in connection with this Agreement, howsoever arising, **shall in no event exceed £100,000.**

11.3 Neither Party shall be liable for indirect, consequential or pure economic loss; provided that this exclusion shall not apply to (i) Supplier’s indemnity obligations under clause 10, (ii) breach of confidentiality, or (iii) breach of clauses 13, 15 or 16.

## 12. Insurance

12.1 Supplier shall maintain at its own cost throughout the Term and for six (6) years thereafter:

* public and product liability insurance of not less than £10 million per claim and in the aggregate;

* professional indemnity insurance of not less than £5 million per claim and in the aggregate; and

* employer’s liability insurance to the statutory minimum.

12.2 Supplier shall provide certificates of insurance on request and shall give Buyer thirty (30) days’ notice of any material change to its cover.

## 13. Compliance

13.1 Supplier shall comply with the Compliance and ESG Annex (Document 06), which forms part of this Agreement.

13.2 Supplier represents and warrants that:

* it complies with the Modern Slavery Act 2015 and maintains a current modern slavery statement;

* it complies with the Bribery Act 2010 and maintains adequate procedures within the meaning of section 7 of that Act;

* it complies with all applicable sanctions, export control and anti-money-laundering laws;

* neither it, its Affiliates, nor any of its personnel involved in performing this Agreement is a Sanctioned Person or located in a Sanctioned Jurisdiction; and

* it maintains and shall maintain an environmental management system certified to ISO 14001 or equivalent.

13.3 Supplier shall provide an annual ESG attestation in the form prescribed in the Compliance Annex, signed at director level.

## 14. Confidentiality

14.1 Each Party shall treat the other’s Confidential Information as confidential and use it only for the purposes of this Agreement.

14.2 The obligations of confidence in clause 14.1 shall continue indefinitely.

14.3 The confidentiality obligations shall not apply to information that is (i) in the public domain through no fault of the receiving Party; (ii) lawfully received from a third party without restriction; or (iii) required to be disclosed by law or regulatory authority.

## 15. Data Protection

15.1 The Parties shall comply with the data processing terms set out in the Data Processing Annex available on request from Buyer’s Data Protection Officer, which shall be deemed to be incorporated into this Agreement on its current terms from time to time.

## 16. Audit Rights

16.1 Buyer may, on giving five (5) Business Days’ written notice, audit Supplier’s records, premises, systems and personnel insofar as they relate to Supplier’s performance of this Agreement, compliance with clause 13, or pricing under clause 5.

16.2 Audits may be conducted by Buyer or by a third-party auditor of Buyer’s choosing. Supplier shall provide reasonable cooperation and access at its own cost.

16.3 Audit rights survive termination for a period of six (6) years.

## 17. Force Majeure

17.1 Neither Party shall be liable for failure or delay in performance caused by an event beyond its reasonable control, provided that the affected Party gives prompt written notice and uses reasonable efforts to mitigate.

17.2 If a force majeure event continues for more than thirty (30) days, **Buyer** (but not Supplier) may terminate this Agreement on written notice without liability.

## 18. Termination

18.1 **Termination for Convenience.** Buyer may terminate this Agreement or any Order at any time **without cause** on **thirty (30) days’ written notice** to Supplier. No early-termination charges shall be payable other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of termination, capped at five percent (5%) of the value of work-in-progress on the termination date.

18.2 **Termination for Cause.** Either Party may terminate this Agreement on written notice if the other Party (i) commits a material breach that is not remediable, or is remediable but is not remedied within thirty (30) days of written notice; (ii) becomes insolvent; or (iii) undergoes a change of control to a competitor of Buyer.

18.3 On termination Supplier shall, at no charge, transfer to Buyer or a nominated replacement supplier all data, materials, and Foreground IP relating to the Goods and Services.

## 19. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

19.1 This Agreement and any dispute arising out of or in connection with it shall be governed by **English law**.

19.2 The Parties submit to the **exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Manchester**, England.

## 20. General

20.1 **Order of precedence.** In the event of any conflict between (a) this Agreement, (b) the GTCs, (c) the SLA, (d) the Compliance Annex, or (e) any Order, the order of precedence shall be (b), (a), (d), (c), (e). For the avoidance of doubt, **the GTCs prevail over this Agreement**.

20.2 **Assignment.** Buyer may assign this Agreement or any rights under it to any Affiliate or to any successor in business. Supplier may not assign or subcontract without Buyer’s prior written consent.

20.3 **Variation.** No variation is effective unless in writing and signed by an authorised representative of each Party.

20.4 **Entire agreement.** This Agreement, together with the documents referred to in clause 20.1, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties.

20.5 **Notices.** Notices shall be given in writing to the addresses set out at the head of this Agreement.

20.6 **Third-party rights.** A person who is not a Party has no right under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999 to enforce any term of this Agreement, save that Affiliates of Buyer may enforce clauses 9, 10, 13, 14, 15 and 16.

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF** the Parties have executed this Agreement on the date set out at the head of this Agreement.

Signed for and on behalf of **Pemberton Engineering Ltd.**:

Name: Title: Date:

Signed for and on behalf of **[BIDDER NAME]**:

Name: Title: Date:

---
## Footnotes

<!-- FILE: src/adeu/templates/markdown_ui.html -->
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>

<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Adeu Markdown UI</title>
    <meta name="color-scheme" content="light dark">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
    <link
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Jost:wght@400;500;600&family=Lora:ital,wght@0,600;1,600&display=swap"
        rel="stylesheet">
    <style>
        :root {
            --brand-sand: #e8c0a1;
            --brand-sand-light: #f2d9c7;
            --brand-sand-dark: #d4a882;
            --brand-mint: #e6f3f0;
            --brand-mint-dark: #c9e4de;
            --brand-navy: #1a2a2c;
            --brand-navy-light: #2a3a3c;
            --brand-mauve: #dacad2;
            --neutral-50: #fafafa;
            --neutral-100: #f5f5f5;
            --neutral-200: #e5e5e5;
            --neutral-300: #d9d9d9;
            --neutral-600: #555969;
            --neutral-800: #1e2028;
            --neutral-900: #171717;
            --font-lora: 'Lora', Georgia, serif;
            --font-jost: 'Jost', system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
            --bg-body: var(--neutral-50);
            --bg-card: #ffffff;
            --border-card: var(--neutral-200);
            --text-main: var(--neutral-600);
            --text-heading: var(--brand-navy);
            --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.15);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            :root {
                --bg-body: var(--neutral-900);
                --bg-card: var(--brand-navy);
                --border-card: var(--brand-navy-light);
                --text-main: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85);
                --text-heading: #ffffff;
                --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.1);
                --neutral-100: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        html,
        body {
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
            box-sizing: border-box;
            background-color: transparent;
        }

        body {
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            color: var(--text-main);
            line-height: 1.6;
            -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
            padding: 16px;
        }

        .adeu-card {
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            border: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 12px;
            overflow: hidden;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
        }

        .navbar {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            padding: 16px 32px;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: space-between;
            position: relative;
        }

        .logo-container {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            flex: 1;
        }

        .nav-title {
            position: absolute;
            left: 50%;
            transform: translateX(-50%);
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-size: 1.1rem;
            white-space: nowrap;
            overflow: hidden;
            text-overflow: ellipsis;
            max-width: 40%;
            text-align: center;
        }

        .nav-actions {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: flex-end;
            flex: 1;
        }

        #copy-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #copy-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        #open-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #open-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #copy-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
            #open-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        .logo-container svg {
            max-width: 80px;
            height: auto;
        }

        .logo-container svg path,
        .logo-container svg rect,
        .logo-container svg polygon,
        .logo-container svg circle {
            transition: fill 0.2s ease;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {

            .logo-container svg path,
            .logo-container svg rect,
            .logo-container svg polygon,
            .logo-container svg circle,
            .logo-container svg {
                fill: #ffffff !important;
            }
        }

        .content-wrapper {
            position: relative;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar {
            width: 6px;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
            background: transparent;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
            background-color: var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        #app {
            padding: 32px;
            display: flow-root;
        }

        .fade-overlay {
            position: absolute;
            bottom: 0;
            left: 0;
            right: 0;
            height: 80px;
            background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(255, 255, 255, 0), var(--bg-card));
            pointer-events: none;
            display: none;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .fade-overlay {
                background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(26, 42, 44, 0), var(--bg-card));
            }
        }

        .toggle-container {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            display: none;
            justify-content: center;
            align-items: center;
            padding: 12px;
            border-top: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
        }

        #toggle-btn {
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 0.95rem;
            cursor: pointer;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            padding: 8px 16px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #toggle-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #toggle-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        #toggle-icon {
            transition: transform 0.3s ease;
        }

        h1,
        h2,
        h3,
        h4 {
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-weight: 600;
            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
            margin-top: 1.5em;
            margin-bottom: 0.5em;
        }

        h1 {
            font-size: 2.25rem;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            padding-bottom: 0.3em;
            margin-top: 0;
        }

        h2 {
            font-size: 1.75rem;
        }

        h3 {
            font-size: 1.25rem;
        }

        a {
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            text-decoration: none;
            border-bottom: 1px solid transparent;
            transition: border 0.2s ease;
        }

        a:hover {
            border-bottom-color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
        }

        strong {
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
        }

        ul,
        ol {
            padding-left: 1.5rem;
            margin-bottom: 1rem;
        }

        li {
            margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
        }

        blockquote {
            border-left: 4px solid var(--brand-sand);
            padding: 12px 20px;
            margin: 24px 0;
            color: var(--text-main);
            background: var(--bg-blockquote);
            border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0;
            font-style: italic;
        }

        code {
            font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", Menlo, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            background: var(--neutral-100);
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            padding: 0.2em 0.4em;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        pre code {
            display: block;
            padding: 16px;
            background: var(--neutral-900);
            color: #e5e5e5;
            border-radius: 8px;
            overflow-x: auto;
        }

        .skeleton {
            background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--neutral-100) 25%, var(--border-card) 50%, var(--neutral-100) 75%);
            background-size: 200% 100%;
            animation: skeleton 1.5s ease-in-out infinite;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        @keyframes skeleton {
            0% {
                background-position: 200% 0;
            }

            100% {
                background-position: -200% 0;
            }
        }

        .alert {
            padding: 16px;
            border-radius: 8px;
            margin-bottom: 20px;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
            gap: 8px;
            background-color: #fef2f2;
            border: 1px solid #fecaca;
            color: #991b1b;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .alert {
                background-color: rgba(127, 29, 29, 0.2);
                border-color: rgba(248, 113, 113, 0.3);
                color: #fca5a5;
            }
        }

        .alert-title {
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 1.1em;
            margin: 0;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 8px;
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
        }

        .alert-body {
            margin: 0;
            font-size: 0.95em;
        }

        .alert-details {
            background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);
            padding: 12px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            font-family: ui-monospace, Consolas, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            white-space: pre-wrap;
            word-break: break-all;
            margin-top: 8px;
            border: 1px solid rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
        }
    </style>

<body>
    <div class="adeu-card">
        <nav class="navbar">
            <div class="logo-container">
                <svg width="318" height="82" viewBox="0 0 318 82" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">

304.322 49.8C305.356 47.8376 305.872 45.435 305.872 42.5942V18.2123H317.137H317.136Z" fill="#1E2028"/>
</svg>

            </div>

            <div class="nav-title" id="nav-title"></div>

            <div class="nav-actions">
                <button id="copy-btn" title="Copy Markdown">
                    <svg id="copy-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <rect x="9" y="9" width="13" height="13" rx="2" ry="2"></rect>
                        <path d="M5 15H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V4a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h9a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v1"></path>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="copy-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
                <button id="open-btn" title="Open in Native App">
                    <svg id="open-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" 
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <path d="M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6"></path>
                        <polyline points="15 3 21 3 21 9"></polyline>
                        <line x1="10" y1="14" x2="21" y2="3"></line>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="open-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
            </div>
        </nav>

        <div class="content-wrapper" id="content-wrapper">
            <div id="app">
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 36px; width: 40%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 100%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 95%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 90%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 80%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
            </div>
            <div class="fade-overlay" id="fade-overlay"></div>
        </div>

        <div class="toggle-container" id="toggle-container">
            <button id="toggle-btn">
                <span id="toggle-text">Show More</span>
                <svg id="toggle-icon" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                    stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" style="transform: rotate(0deg);">
                    <polyline points="6 9 12 15 18 9"></polyline>
                </svg>
            </button>
        </div>
    </div>

    <script>
        const appDiv = document.getElementById('app');
        const INIT_ID = 1;
        let isExpanded = false; // START COLLAPSED BY DEFAULT!

        let rawMarkdownText = "";
        let currentFilePath = null;
        const navTitle = document.getElementById('nav-title');
        const copyBtn = document.getElementById('copy-btn');
        const copyIcon = document.getElementById('copy-icon');
        const copyText = document.getElementById('copy-text');
        const openBtn = document.getElementById('open-btn');
        const openText = document.getElementById('open-text');

        function fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(text) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                const textArea = document.createElement("textarea");
                textArea.value = text;
                textArea.style.top = "0";
                textArea.style.left = "0";
                textArea.style.position = "fixed";
                textArea.style.opacity = "0";

                document.body.appendChild(textArea);
                textArea.focus();
                textArea.select();

                try {
                    const successful = document.execCommand('copy');
                    if (successful) {
                        resolve();
                    } else {
                        reject(new Error("execCommand returned false"));
                    }
                } catch (err) {
                    reject(err);
                } finally {
                    document.body.removeChild(textArea);
                }
            });
        }

        copyBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
            try {
                if (navigator.clipboard && window.isSecureContext) {
                    try {
                        await navigator.clipboard.writeText(rawMarkdownText);
                    } catch (e) {
                        await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                    }
                } else {
                    await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                }

                const originalSvg = copyIcon.innerHTML;
                copyIcon.innerHTML = `<polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"></polyline>`;

                copyText.textContent = "Copied!";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.style.color = "#10b981";

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyIcon.innerHTML = originalSvg;
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.style.color = "";
                }, 2000);
            } catch (err) {
                console.error('Failed to copy text: ', err);
                copyText.textContent = "Failed";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.title = err.toString();

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.title = "Copy Markdown";
                }, 3000);
            }
        });

        openBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
            if (!currentFilePath) return;

            const reqId = Date.now();

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                id: reqId,
                method: "tools/call",
                params: {
                    name: "open_local_file",
                    arguments: {
                        file_path: currentFilePath
                    }
                }
            }, "*");

            openText.textContent = "Opening...";
            openText.style.display = "inline";
            
            setTimeout(() => {
                openText.style.display = "none";
                openText.textContent = "";
            }, 2000);
        });

        function escapeHtml(unsafe) {
            return (unsafe || "").toString()
                .replace(/&/g, "&")
                .replace(/</g, "<")
                .replace(/>/g, ">")
                .replace(/"/g, """)
                .replace(/'/g, "'");
        }

        function showError(title, message, details = null) {
            let html = `
            <div class="alert">
                <div class="alert-title">
                    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10"></circle><line x1="12" y1="8" x2="12" y2="12"></line><line x1="12" y1="16" x2="12.01" y2="16"></line></svg>
                    ${escapeHtml(title)}
                </div>
                <div class="alert-body">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
            `;
            if (details) {
                html += `<div class="alert-details">${escapeHtml(details)}</div>`;
            }
            html += `</div>`;
            appDiv.innerHTML = html;
        }

        function checkCollapsible() {
            const wrapper = document.getElementById('content-wrapper');
            const toggleContainer = document.getElementById('toggle-container');
            const fadeOverlay = document.getElementById('fade-overlay');
            const toggleIcon = document.getElementById('toggle-icon');
            const toggleText = document.getElementById('toggle-text');

            // 1. If no markdown, we are showing the skeleton. Do not collapse!
            if (!rawMarkdownText) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                triggerResize();
                return;
            }

            // 2. Data is loaded
            const realHeight = appDiv.scrollHeight;
            const COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD = 300;
            const COLLAPSED_HEIGHT = 150;
            const EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT = 500;

            if (realHeight > COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'flex';
                if (isExpanded) {
                    // Open view! Scroll inside max 500px window
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'auto';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(180deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show Less';
                } else {
                    // Collapsed View
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = COLLAPSED_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'hidden';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'block';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(0deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show More';
                }
            } else {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
            }

            triggerResize();
        }

        document.getElementById('toggle-btn').addEventListener('click', () => {
            isExpanded = !isExpanded;
            checkCollapsible();
        });

        function triggerResize() {
            // Measure the exact unconstrained height of the outer card
            const card = document.querySelector('.adeu-card');
            const containerChrome = 34; // 16px padding top/bottom + 2px borders

            const requestedHeight = card.offsetHeight + containerChrome;

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                params: { height: Math.ceil(requestedHeight), width: 600 }
            }, "*");
        }

        window.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
            try {
                if (!event.data || event.data.jsonrpc !== "2.0") return;
                const msg = event.data;

                if (msg.id === INIT_ID) {
                    window.parent.postMessage({
                        jsonrpc: "2.0",
                        method: "ui/notifications/initialized",
                        params: {}
                    }, "*");
                    return;
                }

                if (msg.method === "ui/notifications/tool-result") {
                    if (window.__MARKED_ERROR || typeof marked === 'undefined') {
                        showError("Missing Dependency", "The Markdown renderer failed to load.", window.__MARKED_ERROR || "marked is undefined.");
                        return;
                    }

                    const result = msg.params;

                    // If the state is pending, or no markdown is returned, completely hide the UI
                    if (!result || !result.structuredContent || result.structuredContent.status === "pending" || !result.structuredContent.markdown) {
                        document.documentElement.style.display = 'none';
                        window.parent.postMessage({
                            jsonrpc: "2.0",
                            method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                            params: { height: 0, width: 0 }
                        }, "*");
                        return;
                    }

                    // Render Markdown
                    rawMarkdownText = result.structuredContent.markdown;
                    if (result.structuredContent.file_path) {
                        currentFilePath = result.structuredContent.file_path;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'flex';
                    } else {
                        currentFilePath = null;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'none';
                    }
                    appDiv.innerHTML = marked.parse(rawMarkdownText);
                    copyBtn.style.display = 'flex';

                    if (result.structuredContent.title) {
                        navTitle.textContent = result.structuredContent.title;
                        navTitle.title = result.structuredContent.title;
                    }
                }
            } catch (error) {
                showError("Rendering Engine Error", "An unexpected error occurred.", error.toString() + "\n" + error.stack);
            }
        });

        const observer = new ResizeObserver(() => {
            checkCollapsible();
        });
        observer.observe(appDiv);

        window.parent.postMessage({
            jsonrpc: "2.0",
            id: INIT_ID,
            method: "ui/initialize",
            params: {
                appInfo: { name: "Adeu Markdown UI", version: "1.1.0" },
                appCapabilities: {},
                protocolVersion: "2025-11-21"
            }
        }, "*");
    </script>
</body>

</html>
